paper_title: Gallai's path decomposition conjecture for graphs of small maximum degree
summary: Question 1.1 — whether every connected graph that is not an odd semi-clique admits a path decomposition into \lfloor |V(G)|/2 \rfloor paths — remains open in full generality. It has been settled positively for planar graphs (Blanché, Bonamy, Bonichon 2021: every connected planar graph except K_3 and K_5^- decomposes into \lfloor n/2 \rfloor paths) and for 2-degenerate graphs (Anto, Basavaraju 2022: every connected 2-degenerate graph except the triangle decomposes into \lfloor n/2 \rfloor paths). Search results also indicate partial progress for 3-degenerate graphs (2024) but those could not be verified within the call budget.

since_posted.title: Gallai's path decomposition in planar graphs
since_posted.authors: Alexandre Blanché, Marthe Bonamy, Nicolas Bonichon
since_posted.claim: Proves the conjecture for planar graphs: every connected planar graph except K_3 and K_5^- (the only two planar odd semi-cliques) can be decomposed into \lfloor n/2 \rfloor paths.

since_posted.title: Gallai's Path Decomposition for 2-degenerate Graphs
since_posted.claim: Proves the conjecture for 2-degenerate graphs: every connected 2-degenerate graph on n vertices can be decomposed into at most \lfloor n/2 \rfloor paths unless it is a triangle (the only 2-degenerate odd semi-clique).

internal_refs_verified.note: Confirmed: paper by Blanché, Bonamy, Bonichon proves \lfloor n/2 \rfloor decomposition for planar graphs with K_3 and K_5^- as the only exceptions, matching the internal reference description exactly.

search_queries: Gallai path decomposition conjecture "floor n/2" "odd semi-clique" connected graph 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: Question 1.1 has been answered positively for planar graphs and 2-degenerate graphs but is open for general connected graphs. A ScienceDirect paper titled 'Gallai's conjecture and the path number of odd semi-cliques' appeared in search results (doi likely 10.1016/j.disc.2025...) but could not be fetched (HTTP 403). Additional partial results for 3-degenerate graphs were indicated in search results but could not be verified within the 5-call budget.
